habitat: Subtropical/tropical moist lowland forests; mangrove forests; secondary growth; and plantations
story: Explore the fascinating world of the Chestnut Breasted Malkoha (Phaenicophaeus curvirostris). Classified under the Cuculidae family, this bird predominantly inhabits Subtropical/tropical moist lowland forests; mangrove forests; secondary growth; and plantations. The current IUCN classification of Least Concern (LC) underscores the importance of safeguarding its natural range. From its feeding habits to its nesting preferences, the Chestnut Breasted Malkoha is a testament to the intricate web of life. By studying species like the Chestnut Breasted Malkoha, we gain valuable insights into broader ecological trends.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
